hook: You already know how to work with derivatives — now imagine running that process in reverse to recover a function from a rule about its own rate of change. That's exactly what a differential equation asks you to do, and it turns out the universe speaks almost entirely in this language, from population growth to electric circuits. The real question is: given so many different types of these equations, how do you know which tool to reach for?
concept: Every topic in this unit is a strategy for solving a different family of first-order equations — the unifying idea is that the structure of the equation tells you which technique unlocks it, captured formally by the study of equations of the form $F(x, y, y') = 0$.

topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to read a differential equation's structure — its order, linearity, and type — so you can immediately identify which solution method applies.
Master the technique of separating variables to reduce a differential equation into two straightforward integrals, one in $x$ and one in $y$.

topic_roadmap.topic_name: Linear First Order Equations and Integrating Factors
topic_roadmap.description: Apply the integrating factor method to solve any first-order linear equation $y' + P(x)y = Q(x)$, even when separation is impossible.
You will learn to recognize when an equation has a hidden potential function and reconstruct that function to write down the solution directly.
Apply strategic substitutions to transform nonlinear or homogeneous equations into simpler forms that you already know how to solve.
You will learn to use the Existence and Uniqueness Theorem to guarantee — before solving — that an initial value problem has exactly one solution.
